message:
Hey <@&1424461167883194418>, 31 PRs merged this week. Here's what actually matters:

## Weekly Update - 2026-03-14

🔌 **OpenAI Compatibility & New Models**
- You can now use standard OpenAI-compatible formats for our image generation endpoints.
- Added a pile of new models: `dirtberry`, `dirtberry-pro`, `claude-airforce`, and Pruna's image/video stack.
- Shoutout to community provider Seraphyn for bringing `qwen3-tts` online.

⚡ **Infra & Pollen**
- The Seed tier upgrade workflow used to take 25 hours. We optimized it down to a few seconds.
- Pollen refills now happen hourly. Less waiting, more generating.

🔑 **BYOP Everywhere**
- Bring Your Own Provider (BYOP) is becoming the default. It's now wired directly into `catgpt` (which also got a light theme), `ai-dungeon-master`, and `product-packaging-designer`.
- Shipped a new media upload service so apps can handle user assets without the headache.

🎨 **Frontend & Vibes**
- The main site got a visual overhaul: new scene backgrounds, a dynamic palette system, and refreshed logos.
- Fixed `nanobanana` so it actually respects `safe=false` when you ask it to.

Build something weird this weekend.
image_prompt: Cozy 8-bit pixel art scene of a retro RPG quest board standing in a lush digital garden. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and pastel lavender gradient background. Polly, the cute pixel art bee mascot (yellow body, dark brown horizontal stripes, small black legs, two black antennae, black vertical oval eyes, bright pink rectangular blush marks, small happy smile, light blue translucent wings) wearing a tiny builder's hard hat, is hovering and pinning a glowing paper to the board. Next to her, a chunky pixel Monitor Robot with a CRT screen head and stubby arms excitedly holds a glowing API cable. A large wooden pixel signboard at the top reads 'WEEKLY BUZZ' in chunky pixel font. Pinned to the board are three retro UI panels with text: 'OPENAI API', 'HOURLY POLLEN', and 'BYOP'. Code-plants sprout from lime green flower pots at the base of the board. Warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic, readable, and emotionally warm.
reasoning: Translates a massive changelog into four highly scannable sections. Focuses on what the user gains (speed, new models, hourly refills) rather than the engineering it took to get there. The image prompt uses the RPG quest board motif to naturally integrate the requested weekly text and feature callouts.
